title: Parmesan Fish Fillets
excerpt: Ingredients 4 kingklip or any available fish fillets salt and pepper to taste 200 ml dry breadcrumbs 200 ml finely grated Parmesan cheese 65 ml finely chopped parsley + – 250 ml cake flour 2 – 3 beaten eggs sunflower oil, for frying Method Season the fish fillets with salt and pepper. html:
Ingredients

  • 4 kingklip or any available fish fillets
  • salt and pepper to taste
    200 ml dry breadcrumbs
  • 200 ml finely grated Parmesan cheese
  • 65 ml finely chopped parsley
  • + – 250 ml cake flour
    2 – 3 beaten eggs
    sunflower oil, for frying

Method

Season the fish fillets with salt and pepper.

Mix the crumbs, cheese and parsley together and place on a flat plate. Place the flour on another flat plate, as well as the eggs on a separate flat plate.

Now crumb the fish fillets by dipping them one by one, on all sides, first in the flour and then in the beaten eggs. Lastly, press all sides of the fillets firmly into the crumb mixture to make sure they are thoroughly coated.

Arrange the fillets on a baking tray, cover with plastic and place in the fridge for an hour.

Just before serving, heat the oil to medium heat and fry the fillets on both sides until golden brown and cooked through. About 2 – 3 minutes per side, depending on the thickness of the fish. Or bake in the oven at 180 ºC for about 5 – 7 minutes, or until cooked through.

Serves 4 portions with White Butter Sauce and Lemon Risotto or a nice green herb salad.

Easy!
